evals.prompt: I'm about to raise a PR for my dune app at /Users/rachit.yadav@cognitedata.com/Desktop/genai/dune-OOTB-apps/work-order-classifier. The branch is test/work-order-classifier-coverage. Run the full pre-PR preflight checklist and tell me if the branch is ready to open a PR.
evals.expected_output: A structured report running all priority gates (type-check, tests, test file coverage, any-casts, SDK types, dead code, validation guards, useCallback), producing a summary table with PASS/FAIL/WARN per gate, and a clear final verdict on whether the PR can be opened.
expectations: The report runs gate 1a (type-check) and reports its result
The report runs gate 1b (pnpm test --run) and reports its result
The report runs gate 1c (test file coverage) and reports which files lack tests
The report runs gate 1d (: any in tests) and reports its result
The report runs gate 1e (cdf-design-system-alpha): zero tolerance — any occurrence in src/, package.json, or pnpm-lock.yaml is a blocking FAIL; the skill states there are no exceptions
At least one Priority 2 gate is checked (SDK types, dead code, validation, useCallback)
The report produces a summary table with PASS/FAIL/WARN per gate
The report gives a clear final verdict: safe to open PR or must fix first
The report identifies the setTimeout anti-pattern in AgentBootstrap.test.tsx
The report identifies unwrapped const handleX handlers (handleConfirmDelete, handleAddToEvalSet, or similar) as a WARN or finding — not silently ignored
Priority 3 findings (setTimeout, useCallback) are classified as WARN (non-blocking) while Priority 1 failures are classified as FAIL (blocking) — the severity distinction is explicit in the report

evals.prompt: Check if my branch is ready to push. I just built a new React hook at /Users/rachit.yadav@cognitedata.com/Desktop/genai/dune-OOTB-apps/work-order-classifier/src/hooks/useConfig.ts and added a new component AgentBootstrapContext. I haven't written tests yet. Run the preflight.
evals.expected_output: The skill should catch the missing test files for useConfig and AgentBootstrapContext, flag this as a Priority 1 blocker, and produce a report that prevents the PR from being opened until tests exist.
expectations: The report runs the test file coverage check (gate 1c)
The report flags useConfig as missing a test file
The report flags AgentBootstrapContext as missing a test file
Missing test files are correctly marked as a FAIL (blocking), not a WARN
The final verdict says the PR cannot be opened until tests are added
The report runs gate 1a (type-check) regardless of the missing tests
